title: Build, publish, confirm installable + measure coverage baseline
context: After the cold-start deck is authored and the build tool is working, the first end-to-end publication proves the full pipeline: CrowdAnki JSON → .apkg build → publishing channel → installable in Anki and AnkiDroid → coverage baseline measured. This is the M0 capstone that validates all prior work and establishes the baseline metric for measuring curriculum coverage improvement.
objective: Build the cold-start deck to .apkg, publish to the confirmed channel with proper attribution, confirm it is installable in Anki and AnkiDroid, and measure the curriculum-coverage baseline for the chosen subject.
acceptanceCriteria: Deck builds deterministically to .apkg using the code-001 build tool; same input JSON produces same .apkg hash.
Deck is confirmed installable in both Anki (desktop) and AnkiDroid with cards rendering correctly, including images.
Deck published to the confirmed channel (from research-001) with full license, attribution, and changelog; source JSON committed to the repository; semver assigned.
Curriculum-coverage baseline measured for the chosen subject using a reproducible method (named standard, snapshot date, in-scope denominator, cards covered / total).
No SM-2/FSRS scheduling state baked in; deck is scheduler-agnostic.
PR includes build output, publication confirmation (link or screenshot), and coverage baseline report.
output: A merged PR containing: published .apkg artifact, publication confirmation, source JSON committed, semver tag, and a coverage-baseline report (docs/coverage-baseline.md).
